chapters.chapter_summary: Patient was positioned for surgery with appropriate padding. General anesthesia was administered. Initial incision was made on the patient's chest.

Surgical dissection began with the use of forceps and scissors to manipulate tissues around the incision site on the patient's leg. The heart-lung machine was set up and connected to the surgical site.
Cannulae were inserted into the heart and connected to the heart-lung machine. The bypass was established, and the heart-lung machine was used to maintain circulation during the procedure.

Incisions were made on the right coronary artery and the left anterior descending (LAD) artery. Venous grafts were prepared and attached to the right coronary artery, followed by anastomosis procedures connecting the grafts to the marginal branch of the circumflex artery and the LAD.
The surgical team ensured hemostasis by controlling bleeding and inspected the grafts for proper function. The patient was weaned off extracorporeal support systems.
The chest cavity was closed with suturing, and the surgical team secured the incision site.
Preparations for postoperative care were made, including the use of medical equipment to monitor the patient's vital signs and ensure stability before moving the patient out of the operating room.

SOAP.Subjective: I performed a coronary artery bypass grafting on a patient with severe coronary artery disease, specifically highlighting the left main stem.
SOAP.Objective: I identified the need for bypass surgery after reviewing the patient's X-ray images and diagnostic evaluation. I observed the surgical dissection and graft preparation, as well as the insertion of cannulae into the heart and the establishment of bypass.
SOAP.Assessment: I assessed the procedure as successful, with proper anastomosis and graft flow. I identified no leaks in the newly constructed graft and confirmed satisfactory outcomes.
SOAP.Plan: I closed the wound in layers and ensured hemostasis. I prepared the patient for postoperative care, including monitoring vital signs and ensuring stability before moving the patient out of the operating room.
